data.current_summary: Preset types of rides for sandbox
data.current_description: The sandbox-ridetypes endpoint allows you to preset the ridetypes in the region surrounding the specified latitude and longitude to allow testing different scenarios
data.summary_suggestion: Configure available ride types for sandbox testing
data.description_suggestion: Configures the available ride types in a specified geographic region for sandbox testing. Accepts a SandboxRideType object containing ride type definitions and location coordinates (latitude/longitude). Enables testing of different ride type scenarios and availability configurations. Returns the updated ride type configuration for the specified region.
data.reasoning: Original summary is vague about the action being performed. Description lacks clarity on input requirements (location coordinates), what the SandboxRideType schema contains, and what is returned. Added specifics about parameters, purpose for testing, and response to improve AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'SetRideTypeAvailability' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, not_actionable. Original summary is unclear about what action is being performed and conflates driver availability with ride request processing. Description lacks specificity about the ride_type parameter format, what SandboxDriverAvailability contains, location requirements, and response format. Enhanced to clarify the action, parameter usage, input/output schemas, and sandbox testing context for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: Driver availability for processing ride request
data.current_description: Set driver availability for the provided ride_type in the city/region surrounding the specified location
data.summary_suggestion: Set driver availability for a ride type
data.description_suggestion: Sets the driver availability status for a specified ride type in a geographic region for sandbox testing. The ride_type path parameter identifies which ride type to configure (e.g., 'lyft', 'lyft_plus'). Accepts a SandboxDriverAvailability object containing availability settings and location coordinates. Returns the updated driver availability configuration for the specified ride type and region.
data.reasoning: Original summary is unclear about what action is being performed and conflates driver availability with ride request processing. Description lacks specificity about the ride_type parameter format, what SandboxDriverAvailability contains, location requirements, and response format. Enhanced to clarify the action, parameter usage, input/output schemas, and sandbox testing context for better AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Estimate the cost of taking a Lyft between two points.
data.summary_suggestion: Estimate Lyft ride cost between locations
data.description_suggestion: Estimates the cost of a Lyft ride between two specified locations. Requires pickup and dropoff coordinates as input parameters. Returns cost details including estimated fare, currency, and any applicable fees or surcharges. Useful for helping passengers understand pricing before requesting a ride.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description lacks detail about required parameters (pickup/dropoff locations), response structure (cost details, currency, fees), and use case context. Enhanced version clarifies inputs, outputs, and when an AI agent should use this operation.

data.current_description: The drivers endpoint returns a list of nearby drivers' lat and lng at a given location.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of Lyft drivers currently available near a specified location. Requires location coordinates (latitude/longitude) as input. Returns driver locations with their current lat/lng coordinates. This endpoint helps determine driver availability and proximity before requesting a ride. Requires Client Authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary is vague about the action. Description lacks clarity on required input parameters, authentication requirements, and practical use case. data.current_description: The ETA endpoint lets you know how quickly a Lyft driver can come get you
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the estimated time of arrival (ETA) for a Lyft driver to reach a specified pickup location. Requires location coordinates as input parameters. Returns estimated pickup time in minutes or seconds. Helps passengers understand wait times before confirming a ride request. Requires Client Authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary is incomplete - doesn't specify it's about driver arrival time. Description is informal and lacks specifics about required inputs (location), output format (time units), and authentication. data.current_description: The v1 of this endpoint returns the user's ID, v2 will return more general info about the user. We require authentication for this endpoint, so we extract the user ID from the access token.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the authenticated user's profile information. Returns the user's ID and general account details extracted from the access token. Requires User Authentication. This endpoint provides basic user identification and profile data for the currently authenticated passenger.
data.reasoning: Summary is acceptable. Description is confusing with version references and implementation details that don't help AI agents. Improved version removes version-specific language, clarifies what data is returned, emphasizes authentication requirement, and explains the practical purpose for AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Get a list of past & current rides for this passenger.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of all past and current rides for the authenticated passenger. Returns ride details including ride status, timestamps, locations, driver information, and costs. Requires User Authentication. Useful for passengers to review their ride history and track ongoing rides.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic. Description lacks detail about what ride information is included in the response (status, timestamps, locations, costs) and authentication requirements. data.current_description: Request a Lyft come pick you up at the given location.
data.description_suggestion: Requests a Lyft ride from a specified pickup location. Requires authenticated user and ride request parameters including pickup location and ride type. Returns a RideDetail object with the ride ID, status, estimated pickup time, and driver information. Creates a new ride record and initiates driver matching. Requires User Authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description lacks specifics about required input parameters (ride type, location format), response structure (RideDetail object, ride ID), and side effects (initiates driver matching, creates ride record). message: Operation 'CancelRide' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary has grammatical issues ('a ongoing' should be 'an active'). Description lacks clarity about what is returned, potential side effects (cancellation fees), and state requirements. Added details about request body, response content, cancellation penalties, and state constraints for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Cancel a ongoing ride which was requested earlier by providing the ride id.
data.description_suggestion: Cancels an active ride that was previously requested. Requires the ride ID as a path parameter and a cancellation request body. Returns the updated ride details with canceled status. Note: Cancellation may incur a penalty fee depending on the ride's current status and timing. The ride must be in an active state (not already dropped off or canceled).
data.reasoning: Original summary has grammatical issues ('a ongoing' should be 'an active'). Description lacks clarity about what is returned, potential side effects (cancellation fees), and state requirements. Added details about request body, response content, cancellation penalties, and state constraints for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'SetRideDestination' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and actionable. Description needs enhancement to specify the response format (RideDetail), clarify that this affects ride estimates, and better structure the constraints for AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: Update the destination of the ride
data.current_description: Add or update the ride's destination. Note that the ride must still be active (not droppedOff or canceled), and that destinations on Lyft Line rides can not be changed.
data.description_suggestion: Adds or updates the destination for an active ride. Accepts a Location object in the request body and returns the updated RideDetail object. The ride must be in an active state (not dropped off or canceled). Lyft Line rides do not support destination changes. This operation may affect the ride's estimated cost and duration.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description needs enhancement to specify the response format (RideDetail), clarify that this affects ride estimates, and better structure the constraints for AI agent decision-making.

data.current_summary: Get the receipt of the rides.
data.current_description: Get the receipt information of a processed ride by providing the ride id. Receipts will only be available to view once the payment has been processed. In the case of canceled ride, cancellation penalty is included if applicable.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve the receipt for a completed ride
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the receipt information for a processed ride using the ride ID. Returns receipt details including charges, fees, and payment breakdown. Receipts are only available after payment processing is complete. For canceled rides, the receipt includes any applicable cancellation penalties. The ride must be in a completed or canceled state.
data.reasoning: Original summary has grammatical error ('receipt of the rides' is unclear). Description lacks specificity about what receipt data is returned. Enhanced to clarify response content, state requirements, and handling of different ride outcomes for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: The ride types endpoint returns information about what kinds of Lyft rides you can request at a given location.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve available ride types at a location
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the available ride types that can be requested at a specified location. Requires location parameters (latitude and longitude). Returns a list of RideTypeEnum values with details about each ride type including pricing information, vehicle details, and availability. This endpoint helps determine which ride options are available before requesting a ride.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description mentions location but doesn't specify required parameters or response format. Enhanced to clarify required location inputs, response structure (list of ride types), and use case context for AI agents.

message: Operation 'SetPrimeTime' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary lacks context that this is sandbox-specific. Description doesn't specify request/response formats or clarify that this only affects sandbox operations. Enhanced to include request schema reference, response behavior, and explicit sandbox context for AI agent clarity.

data.current_description: Preset a Prime Time percentage in the region surrounding the specified location. This Prime Time percentage will be applied when requesting cost, or when requesting a ride in sandbox mode.
data.summary_suggestion: Set Prime Time percentage for sandbox testing
data.description_suggestion: Sets a Prime Time percentage for the region surrounding a specified location in sandbox mode. Accepts a SandboxPrimetime request object containing location and percentage values. The configured Prime Time percentage will be applied to all subsequent cost estimates and ride requests in that region within the sandbox environment. Returns confirmation of the updated Prime Time setting. This is a sandbox-only operation for testing surge pricing scenarios.
data.reasoning: Original summary lacks context that this is sandbox-specific. Description doesn't specify request/response formats or clarify that this only affects sandbox operations. Enhanced to include request schema reference, response behavior, and explicit sandbox context for AI agent clarity.

message: Operation 'SetRideStatus' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, unclear_purpose, missing_output_details. Original summary and description are vague and lack clarity about the operation's purpose. 'Propagate' is unclear terminology. Enhanced to use clearer action language ('advance'), specify request/response formats, explain valid status transitions, and clarify sandbox-only context for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Propagate a sandbox-ride through various ride status
data.summary_suggestion: Advance sandbox ride through status lifecycle
data.description_suggestion: Advances a sandbox ride through its lifecycle by updating its status. Accepts a SandboxRideStatus request object specifying the target ride status. Returns the updated RideDetail object reflecting the new status. Valid status transitions follow the ride lifecycle (requested → accepted → arrived → in_progress → droppedOff or canceled). This sandbox-only operation enables testing of different ride scenarios and state transitions without actual ride requests.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are vague and lack clarity about the operation's purpose. 'Propagate' is unclear terminology. Enhanced to use clearer action language ('advance'), specify request/response formats, explain valid status transitions, and clarify sandbox-only context for better AI agent interpretability.
